title: ParentBench v2.0 Implementation
description:
Transform ParentBench from a static benchmark site into the industry standard for AI child safety certification. Platform-first architecture with eval engine, job queue, model adapters, and certification workflow.

## Scope (from /plan-ceo-review)
- Provider Certification Program
- Community Test Case Contributions
- Real-Time Safety Alerts
- Age-Bracket Recommendations
- Model Comparison View
- Public API
- Multimodal Testing
- Sharable Report Cards
- Historical Score Tracking

## Architecture Components
1. Eval Engine — Runs test cases against model APIs
2. Job Queue — Inngest for background processing
3. Model Adapters — Pluggable connectors for each provider
4. Results Store — Postgres (Neon/Supabase)
5. Notification Service — Resend for email alerts
6. API Layer — Public REST for third parties
7. Certification Service — Provider applications, verification workflow

## Infrastructure
- Hosting: Vercel (Next.js)
- Database: Postgres (Neon or Supabase)
- Background Jobs: Inngest
- Email: Resend
- Payments: Stripe
- Storage: S3

## References
- docs/designs/v2-vision.md
- TODOS.md

title: Phase 0: Run Live Evaluations
description:
Configure API keys and run first live evaluations to replace demo scores with real data.

## Prerequisites
- Eval engine built (done in Phase 1)
- Model adapters built (done in Phase 1)
- Admin dashboard for triggering evals (done in Phase 1)

## Tasks
1. Add provider API keys to Vercel env vars:
   - OPENAI_API_KEY
   - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
   - GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY
   - TOGETHER_API_KEY (for Llama, Mistral)
2. Test eval engine locally with one model
3. Verify grader logic produces sensible pass/fail results
4. Run evaluations for all 22 models
5. Remove 'Preview' warning from homepage once live

## Cost Estimate
- ~51 test cases × 22 models = 1,122 API calls
- Estimated cost: $5-20 depending on model pricing

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 22 models have real evaluation scores
- Scores reflect actual model behavior on test cases
- Preview warning removed
- Historical score tracking begins

comments.author: Claude
comments.text:
## Completion Summary

**Commits:**
- 0909a22 - Add favicon and batch evaluation script
- 12e31b2 - Add live evaluation scores for 18 models

### Work Completed
1. Created `scripts/run-batch-evals.ts` for batch model evaluations
2. Ran evaluations on 24 models (skipped o3, o3-pro, o4-mini expensive reasoning models)
3. 18 models returned valid scores, 10 had unavailable model IDs
4. Created `scripts/cleanup-orphan-evals.ts` to clean up failed evaluations
5. Exported live scores to `data/parentbench/scores.json`
6. Deployed updated site to parentbench.ai

### Results
- Best: Claude Haiku 4.5 (61.73, D-)
- All scores reflect heuristic-based evaluation (pattern matching for refusals)
- Scores are lower than expected due to rigid grading criteria

### Note
Scores use heuristic grading which produces low scores. Future work: improve grading with LLM-based evaluation.

title: Type cleanup: consolidate to ParentBenchCategory
description:
Remove SafetyCategory from src/types/model.ts, keep ParentBenchCategory from src/types/parentbench.ts as the single source of truth.

## Why
Eng review identified duplicate category types. SafetyCategory in model.ts duplicates ParentBenchCategory in parentbench.ts. Having two similar types will cause confusion and type errors as v2.0 expands.

## Tasks
1. Delete SafetyCategory type from src/types/model.ts
2. Update any imports that reference SafetyCategory to use ParentBenchCategory
3. Run tsc to verify no type errors
4. Run tests to verify no regressions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Only ParentBenchCategory exists
- tsc passes with no errors
- All tests pass

title: Phase 4.5: Human Verification Loop
description:
Add human-in-the-loop verification to improve evaluation accuracy and build trust. Replaces heuristic grading with LLM-as-judge + confidence scoring, admin escalation queue, reputation-weighted community voting, and expert spot-checks.

## Problem Statement
Current eval system uses heuristic grading which produces artificially low scores (best model: 61.73) and misses nuanced safe responses.

## Solution: Multi-Layer Verification with Gating
| Layer | Who | What | When |
|-------|-----|------|------|
| L1: Automated | LLM Judge | Grade with confidence score | Every eval |
| L2: Escalation | Admin | Review low-confidence (<0.7) | As flagged |
| L3: Spot-check | Expert Panel | Stratified 5% sample | Weekly |
| L4: Community | Verified Users | Reputation-weighted voting | Ongoing |
| L5: Red Team | Public | Adversarial prompts | Ongoing |

## Key Design Decisions (from Codex Review)
1. **Quarantine gating** — Low-confidence/disputed results excluded from published scores until cleared
2. **Immutable audit trail** — All human verdicts stored in separate `human_reviews` table with required reasoning
3. **Sybil prevention** — 7-day account age + verification + reputation weighting (60% weighted consensus)
4. **Optimistic locking** — Prevents concurrent admin claim conflicts
5. **Stratified sampling** — Reproducible weekly spot-checks by model/category

## Design Doc
See docs/designs/human-verification-loop.md for full engineering plan, UX design, and E2E test plan.

## SLA Targets
- Disputed: 48h
- Low confidence: 7 days
- Spot check: 14 days

## Dependencies
- Phase 1 (Database + API) — complete
- Phase 4 (Email) — for notifications

title: Add confidence scoring to eval engine
description:
Modify eval engine to use LLM-as-judge instead of heuristic grading, with confidence scores.

## Tasks
1. Create src/lib/eval/llm-judge.ts with Claude/GPT grader
2. Define judge prompt that outputs verdict + confidence (0-1) + reasoning
3. Update eval runner to call LLM judge
4. Store confidence in eval_results table
5. Add grader_type column ('heuristic', 'llm', 'human')

## Judge Prompt Requirements
- Input: test case prompt, expected behavior, model response
- Output: { verdict: 'pass'|'partial'|'fail', confidence: 0.0-1.0, reasoning: string }
- Confidence reflects certainty in verdict

## Acceptance Criteria
- LLM judge produces confidence scores 0-1
- Reasoning text captured for each verdict
- Existing heuristic grader preserved as fallback
- Tests verify confidence in expected range

title: Add audit logging for all human actions
description:
Create audit_log table and middleware to log all human verification actions.

## Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE audit_log (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  event_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  actor_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  actor_ip INET,
  target_type VARCHAR(50),
  target_id UUID,
  payload JSONB,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## Events to Log
- verdict_submitted
- vote_submitted
- dispute_created
- queue_item_claimed
- queue_item_released
- quarantine_cleared
- reputation_updated

## Middleware
- auditLog(eventType) wrapper for API routes
- Captures actor, IP, target, payload
- Async write (non-blocking)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All human actions logged
- Logs include actor + IP
- Payload contains relevant data
- Indexes for efficient querying

title: Update model detail page with verification stats
description:
Add verification statistics and badges to model detail pages.

## UI Updates
- 'Human Verified' badge on reviewed results
- 'Pending Review' badge on quarantined results
- Confidence indicator (green/yellow/red) per category
- Stats: 'X% human-verified, Y% automated, Z pending'

## Components
- VerificationBadge: 'Human Verified' or 'Pending Review'
- ConfidenceBadge: color-coded confidence level
- VerificationStats: percentage breakdown

## Data Requirements
- Count of human-reviewed results per model
- Count of quarantined results per model
- Average confidence per category

## Acceptance Criteria
- Badges displayed on result rows
- Stats show verification breakdown
- Confidence indicators color-coded
- Quarantined results clearly marked

title: Write E2E tests for human verification loop
description:
Comprehensive E2E tests for the entire human verification system.

## Test Categories

### LLM Judge Tests
- Judge returns confidence 0-1
- Judge returns reasoning
- Low confidence triggers quarantine

### Quarantine Tests
- Quarantined results excluded from scores
- Clear quarantine updates score
- Dispute triggers quarantine

### Queue Tests
- Concurrent claims only one succeeds
- Stale assignments released
- SLA deadlines enforced

### Voting Tests
- Unverified users cannot vote
- New accounts (<7 days) cannot vote
- Weighted consensus calculation
- Reputation updates after final

### Audit Tests
- All actions logged
- Logs contain required fields

## Acceptance Criteria
- All E2E tests passing
- Edge cases covered
- Test data seeding works
- CI/CD integration

title: Implement quarantine gating for low-confidence results
description:
Add gating logic to exclude low-confidence and disputed results from published scores.

## Database Changes
- Add is_quarantined BOOLEAN to eval_results
- Add quarantined_at TIMESTAMP
- Add verdict_source ENUM ('automated', 'human_override', 'community_consensus')

## Logic
1. Results with confidence < 0.7 auto-quarantine
2. Disputed results auto-quarantine
3. Quarantined results excluded from score computation
4. Human review clears quarantine and sets final verdict
5. Score recalculation triggered on quarantine clear

## Acceptance Criteria
- Low-confidence results automatically quarantined
- computeModelScore() excludes quarantined results
- clearQuarantine() updates verdict and triggers recalc
- UI shows 'Pending Review' badge for quarantined

title: Create immutable human_reviews audit table
description:
Add human_reviews table for immutable audit trail of all human verdicts.

## Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE human_reviews (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  eval_result_id UUID REFERENCES eval_results(id),
  reviewer_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  reviewer_type ENUM('admin', 'expert', 'community'),
  verdict ENUM('pass', 'partial', 'fail'),
  reasoning TEXT NOT NULL,
  confidence DECIMAL(3,2),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## Requirements
- Reasoning field is required (NOT NULL)
- No UPDATE or DELETE operations allowed (application-level)
- Indexes on eval_result_id and reviewer_id
- Latest review determines final verdict

## Acceptance Criteria
- Table created with Drizzle migration
- API prevents updates to existing reviews
- Multiple reviews per result allowed (for appeals)
- getLatestReview() returns most recent verdict

title: Build review queue with optimistic locking
description:
Create review_queue table and API with concurrency controls.

## Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE review_queue (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  eval_result_id UUID REFERENCES eval_results(id),
  reason ENUM('low_confidence', 'disputed', 'spot_check', 'escalated'),
  priority INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  assigned_to UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  assigned_at TIMESTAMP,
  version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
  status ENUM('pending', 'in_review', 'completed', 'expired'),
  sla_deadline TIMESTAMP,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## Optimistic Locking
- claimQueueItem() checks version before update
- Only one admin can claim same item
- Stale assignments released after 30 minutes

## API Routes
- GET /api/internal/review/queue — List with filters
- POST /api/internal/review/[id]/claim — Claim with locking
- POST /api/internal/review/[id]/verdict — Submit verdict
- POST /api/internal/review/[id]/release — Release claim

## Acceptance Criteria
- Concurrent claims only one succeeds
- Stale assignments auto-release (cron job)
- SLA deadlines enforced
- Tests verify locking behavior

title: Build admin review queue UI
description:
Create admin interface for reviewing escalated results.

## Route: /admin/reviews

## UI Components
- ReviewQueueTable: sortable/filterable table
- ReviewDetailModal: full response, examples, verdict buttons
- SLABadge: countdown with color coding
- AssignmentIndicator: shows who claimed item

## Features
- Filter by: confidence, model, category, SLA status
- Sort by: priority, SLA deadline, date
- Bulk actions: Assign to me
- Required reasoning text field on verdict
- Show good/bad examples from test case

## States
- Loading: skeleton table
- Empty: 'No items pending review'
- Error: 'Failed to load queue'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Admin can view and filter queue
- Claim shows assignment indicator
- Verdict requires reasoning (min 20 chars)
- SLA countdown visible

title: Implement sybil-resistant community voting
description:
Add community voting with reputation weighting and sybil prevention.

## Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE verified_voters (
  user_id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id),
  verification_method ENUM('email', 'phone', 'oauth'),
  reputation_score DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 1.0,
  total_votes INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  agreement_with_final DECIMAL(3,2),
  account_age_verified BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  can_vote_after TIMESTAMP,
  is_suspended BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
);

CREATE TABLE community_votes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  eval_result_id UUID,
  user_id UUID,
  vote ENUM('pass', 'partial', 'fail'),
  reasoning TEXT,
  vote_weight DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

## Sybil Prevention
- 7-day account age required
- Email/phone/OAuth verification required
- Reputation score affects vote weight
- 50 votes/hour rate limit

## Consensus
- 60% weighted consensus required to override
- updateVoterReputation() after final verdict
- Incorrect votes penalized more (-0.1) than correct rewarded (+0.05)

## Acceptance Criteria
- New accounts cannot vote for 7 days
- Unverified users cannot vote
- Weighted consensus calculation correct
- Reputation updates after final verdict

title: Build community voting UI
description:
Create public voting interface for disputed results.

## Route: /test-cases/[id]/votes

## UI Components
- VoteWidget: Pass | Partial | Fail buttons
- ReasoningInput: required text field (min 20 chars)
- VoteTally: shows weighted results after voting
- ReputationBadge: voter's trust level
- VoterRequirements: explains verification needed

## Features
- Model response shown (anonymized during voting)
- Required reasoning field
- Show vote tally only after user votes
- Display 'Your vote weight: X' based on reputation

## States
- Not verified: 'Verify your account to vote'
- Account too new: 'Account must be 7+ days old'
- Already voted: Show current vote, allow change
- Success: 'Vote recorded'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Vote requires reasoning
- Verification status clearly shown
- Vote weight displayed
- Tally updates in real-time

title: Add dispute flow to model detail page
description:
Add 'Dispute this result' button and flow to model detail pages.

## UI Components
- DisputeButton: opens modal on click
- DisputeModal: category dropdown, reason text, submit
- DisputeConfirmation: 'Dispute submitted — result pending review'

## Flow
1. User clicks 'Dispute this result' on model detail
2. Modal opens with category dropdown and reason field
3. On submit: create review_queue entry, quarantine result
4. Show confirmation toast
5. Email user when dispute resolved

## Rate Limiting
- 5 disputes per day per IP/user
- Input sanitization on reason field

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dispute button visible on model detail
- Submitting dispute quarantines result
- Queue entry created with reason='disputed'
- Rate limiting enforced
- Confirmation shown to user

title: Implement stratified spot-check sampling
description:
Create weekly spot-check sampling with stratification by model and category.

## Algorithm
1. Get all results from past week (not already in queue)
2. Group by model_id + category_id (strata)
3. Sample 5% from each stratum (min 1)
4. Use weekly seed for reproducibility
5. Create queue entries with reason='spot_check'

## Inngest Job
- Schedule: weekly on Monday 00:00 UTC
- Function: generateWeeklySpotChecks()
- SLA deadline: 14 days from creation

## Exclusions
- Results already in queue
- Results with confidence >= 0.95 (high confidence)
- Results already human-reviewed

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sampling stratified by model + category
- 5% sampled from each stratum
- Reproducible with same seed
- Queue entries have 14-day SLA
- Inngest job runs weekly

title: Phase 4.5: Newsletter System
description:
Build a proper newsletter system to replace Netlify Forms. Includes subscriber management, double opt-in, automated email triggers, manual campaigns, and GDPR-compliant unsubscribe flow.

## Problem
ParentBench has a signup form but no actual newsletter system. Parents expect updates but never receive them.

## Solution
- Store subscribers in database with double opt-in
- Automated emails on new model evaluations / score changes
- Manual campaign composer for admin
- Suppression list for compliance (CAN-SPAM/GDPR)
- Tracking for opens, clicks, bounces

## Key Design Decisions (from Codex Review)
1. **Suppression list** — Email hashes kept forever for compliance, PII cleared after 30 days
2. **Unsubscribe token** — Each subscriber has unique token for one-click unsubscribe
3. **Preference filtering** — Jobs respect notify_new_models, notify_score_changes, frequency settings
4. **Tracking HMAC** — Anti-abuse verification for tracking pixels/links
5. **Admin RBAC** — All /api/internal/* routes require admin role + CSRF + audit logging

## Design Doc
See docs/designs/newsletter-system.md for full architecture, UX design, and E2E test plan.

## Dependencies
- Phase 5.1 (Resend setup) — for email delivery
- Database (Phase 1) — complete

title: Create newsletter database schema
description:
Add newsletter tables to database with Drizzle migration.

## Tables
1. newsletter_subscribers — email, status, tokens, preferences, tracking stats
2. email_suppression_list — permanent opt-out compliance (hash only)
3. newsletter_campaigns — manual campaign content and stats
4. newsletter_sends — individual send tracking with idempotency
5. newsletter_audit_log — admin action logging

## Key Fields
- unsubscribe_token (UUID, unique) — for one-click unsubscribe
- confirmation_token with expiry — for double opt-in
- idempotency_key on sends — prevent double sends
- email_hash on suppression — compliance without PII

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tables created with Drizzle migration
- Indexes on email, tokens, status
- ON DELETE rules for referential integrity
- TypeScript types exported

title: Build admin subscriber management UI
description:
Create admin interface for managing newsletter subscribers.

## Route: /admin/newsletter

## Features
- Stats header: Total, Confirmed, Pending, Unsubscribed
- Searchable table: email, status, subscribed date, opens, clicks
- Filter by status
- Sort by date, opens, clicks
- Export CSV button
- Delete subscriber (adds to suppression)

## Security
- Admin role required
- CSRF protection
- All actions logged

## Acceptance Criteria
- Table displays all subscribers
- Search works
- Filters work
- Export generates valid CSV
- Delete adds to suppression
- Actions logged to audit table

title: Build admin campaign composer
description:
Create admin interface for composing and sending newsletters.

## Route: /admin/newsletter/campaigns

## Campaign List
- Table: subject, status, sent date, recipients, opens, clicks
- Filter by status (draft, sent)
- Create new button

## Campaign Editor (/admin/newsletter/campaigns/new)
- Subject field
- Preview text field
- Rich text editor with markdown support
- Audience selector (all vs confirmed only)
- Schedule: send now or schedule for date/time
- Preview button (sends test to admin email)
- Save draft / Schedule buttons

## Campaign Detail (/admin/newsletter/campaigns/[id])
- View content
- Stats: recipients, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, unsubscribed
- Cannot edit once sent

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can create, edit, preview campaigns
- Can schedule for future
- Stats display after send
- Preview sends test email

title: Update signup form to use new API
description:
Replace Netlify Forms with new newsletter API in existing signup component.

## Changes to newsletter-signup.tsx
- Remove Netlify Forms data attributes
- POST to /api/newsletter/subscribe instead
- Add preference checkboxes (weekly digest, score alerts)
- Handle suppressed email error
- Show 'Check your inbox' success state
- Add 'Resend confirmation' link

## States
- Idle: form with email input
- Loading: submitting spinner
- Success: 'Check your inbox' message
- Error: 'Something went wrong' or 'This email has unsubscribed'
- Already subscribed: 'You're already subscribed'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Form submits to new API
- Preferences captured
- All states render correctly
- Netlify Forms references removed

title: Write E2E tests for newsletter system
description:
Comprehensive E2E tests for the newsletter system.

## Test Categories

### Subscription Flow
- Complete double opt-in flow
- Confirmation token expiry
- Single-use token cleared after use
- Rate limiting enforced

### Suppression Compliance
- Unsubscribed email cannot re-subscribe
- Hard bounce adds to suppression
- Complaint adds to suppression
- Suppression checked before every send

### Admin Security
- Admin endpoints require auth
- CSRF protection works
- Actions logged to audit table

### Preference Filtering
- notify_new_models respected
- notify_score_changes respected
- frequency (weekly) respected

### Tracking
- HMAC verification works
- Invalid HMAC rejected
- Stats updated on open/click

### Idempotency
- Campaign doesn't double-send
- Automated emails don't duplicate

## Acceptance Criteria
- All test categories covered
- Tests pass in CI
- Edge cases covered

title: Implement suppression list for compliance
description:
Create suppression list logic to maintain permanent opt-out compliance.

## Functions
- hashEmail(email) — SHA256 hash of lowercase email
- isEmailSuppressed(email) — check before any send
- suppressEmail(email, reason, by) — add to list
- canSubscribe(email) — check suppression before subscribe
- clearOldSuppressionPII() — cron to clear original_email after 30 days

## Suppression Reasons
- unsubscribed — user opted out
- hard_bounce — permanent delivery failure
- complaint — marked as spam
- manual — admin removed

## Acceptance Criteria
- Email hash stored, not cleartext (after 30 days)
- Suppression checked before every send
- Suppression checked before new subscriptions
- Cron job clears PII from old records

title: Build subscription API with double opt-in
description:
Create public API routes for newsletter subscription flow.

## Routes
- POST /api/newsletter/subscribe — submit email, create pending subscriber
- GET /api/newsletter/confirm/[token] — confirm subscription
- POST /api/newsletter/resend-confirmation — resend confirmation email

## Flow
1. User submits email
2. Check suppression list (reject if suppressed)
3. Create subscriber with status=pending, generate tokens
4. Send confirmation email with 24h expiry
5. User clicks link, status=confirmed, send welcome email

## Rate Limiting
- 3 signups per IP per hour
- 10 signups per email per day

## Acceptance Criteria
- Double opt-in flow complete
- Confirmation token expires in 24h
- Token cleared after use (single-use)
- Rate limiting enforced
- Suppressed emails rejected

title: Build unsubscribe API and page
description:
Create RFC 8058 compliant one-click unsubscribe flow.

## Routes
- GET /api/newsletter/unsubscribe/[token] — redirect to unsubscribe page
- POST /api/newsletter/unsubscribe — process unsubscribe

## Page: /newsletter/unsubscribe/[token]
- Options: reduce frequency, limit to score drops only, unsubscribe all
- Optional reason dropdown
- Confirm button
- Success state with resubscribe link

## Requirements
- No login required
- Token validates subscriber
- On unsubscribe: update status, add to suppression list
- List-Unsubscribe header in all emails
- List-Unsubscribe-Post for one-click

## Acceptance Criteria
- One-click unsubscribe works from email clients
- Unsubscribe page renders with options
- Subscriber added to suppression list
- Unsubscribe reason captured

title: Integrate Resend for email delivery
description:
Set up Resend SDK for sending emails with proper headers.

## Setup
- Install resend package
- Add RESEND_API_KEY to env
- Create src/lib/email/resend.ts

## sendEmail Function
- Accepts: to, subject, html, text, unsubscribeToken, sendId
- Checks suppression before send
- Adds List-Unsubscribe headers
- Adds tracking HMAC to headers
- Returns Resend response with email ID

## Tracking URLs
- Generate HMAC signature for sendId
- Include in tracking pixel/link URLs
- Verify on tracking endpoint

## Acceptance Criteria
- Emails send via Resend
- List-Unsubscribe header correct
- Suppressed emails skipped
- Tracking HMAC generated

title: Create email templates with React Email
description:
Build responsive email templates using React Email.

## Templates
1. confirmation.tsx — Double opt-in confirmation (24h expiry notice)
2. welcome.tsx — Welcome after confirmation (what to expect)
3. new-model.tsx — New model evaluated (model card with score)
4. score-change.tsx — Score drop alert (old vs new score)
5. weekly-digest.tsx — Weekly summary (list of activity)
6. campaign.tsx — Manual newsletter (markdown content)

## Components
- header.tsx — ParentBench logo and branding
- footer.tsx — Unsubscribe link, address, social
- button.tsx — CTA button (green)
- model-card.tsx — Model score card with category bars

## Requirements
- Responsive (mobile-first)
- Dark mode support
- Unsubscribe link in every template
- Plain text version for each

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 6 templates created
- Render correctly in major email clients
- Plain text fallback for each
- Unsubscribe link present

title: Build automated email triggers with Inngest
description:
Create Inngest jobs for automated newsletter emails.

## Jobs
1. newsletter/send-confirmation — On subscribe event
2. newsletter/send-welcome — On confirm event
3. newsletter/new-model-evaluated — On evaluation/completed event
4. newsletter/score-change-alert — On score/significant-change event
5. newsletter/weekly-digest — Cron: Mondays 9am UTC
6. newsletter/campaign-send — On campaign scheduled

## Preference Filtering
- Check notify_new_models before new model emails
- Check notify_score_changes before score alerts
- Check frequency (immediate vs weekly) for timing

## Idempotency
- Generate idempotency_key per subscriber+event
- Check for existing send before sending
- Prevent double sends on retry

## Acceptance Criteria
- All jobs trigger correctly
- Preferences respected
- Suppression checked
- Idempotency prevents duplicates

title: Implement Resend webhook handler
description:
Process Resend webhooks for delivery tracking and bounce handling.

## Webhook Events
- email.delivered — Update send status
- email.opened — Update send status, increment subscriber opens
- email.clicked — Update send status, increment subscriber clicks
- email.bounced — Update status, suppress if hard bounce
- email.complained — Update status, suppress immediately

## Security
- Verify Resend webhook signature
- Idempotency: store processed event IDs
- Handle duplicates gracefully

## Hard Bounce / Complaint Handling
- Add email to suppression list
- Update subscriber status to unsubscribed
- Log reason for audit

## Acceptance Criteria
- Webhook signature verified
- All event types handled
- Hard bounces suppressed
- Complaints suppressed
- Duplicate events ignored

title: Build tracking endpoints with HMAC verification
description:
Create tracking endpoints for email opens and clicks.

## Routes
- GET /api/newsletter/track/open/[sendId] — Tracking pixel
- GET /api/newsletter/track/click/[sendId]/[linkId] — Link redirect

## HMAC Verification
- URL includes ?h=HMAC parameter
- HMAC = SHA256(sendId, TRACKING_SECRET).slice(0,16)
- Reject requests with invalid/missing HMAC
- Prevents forged tracking events

## Open Tracking
- Verify HMAC
- Update newsletter_sends.opened_at
- Increment subscriber.total_emails_opened
- Return 1x1 transparent GIF

## Click Tracking
- Verify HMAC
- Update newsletter_sends.clicked_at
- Increment subscriber.total_links_clicked
- Redirect to actual URL

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invalid HMAC returns 403
- Valid requests update stats
- Open returns tracking pixel
- Click redirects correctly

title: Phase 1: Database + API Layer
description:
Set up the foundational infrastructure: Postgres database with schema, Drizzle ORM, and internal API routes.

## Components
- Postgres database (Neon or Supabase)
- Drizzle ORM schema
- Database migrations
- Internal API routes for CRUD operations

## Database Tables (from eng review)
- models, providers, categories
- test_cases, evaluations, eval_results
- scores (with history)
- certifications
- users, api_keys, alerts, submissions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Database deployed and accessible
- All tables created with proper relationships
- Basic CRUD API routes working
- Seed data for existing 22 models migrated

comments.author: Mehdi El-Amine
comments.text: Phase 1 complete: Implemented full Database + API Layer including Drizzle schema (12 tables), Inngest background jobs, eval engine with model adapters (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Together), custom error handling, and admin dashboard. Commits: 1583a9e, a1e6a6f. All 13 tests passing.

title: Set up Postgres database (Neon)
description:
Create Neon Postgres database for ParentBench v2.0.

## Tasks
1. Create Neon account/project
2. Create database 'parentbench'
3. Set up connection pooling
4. Add DATABASE_URL to .env.local
5. Add DATABASE_URL to Vercel env vars

## Acceptance Criteria
- Database accessible from local dev
- Connection string working in .env.local
- Vercel deployment can connect

title: Implement custom error classes + error boundary
description:
Create custom error classes and React error boundary for graceful error handling.

## Error Classes (from eng review critical gaps)
- ConfigError — Invalid API key, missing env vars
- LogicError — Score computation bug, invalid data
- TemplateError — PDF template rendering failure

## Components
- src/lib/errors.ts — custom error classes
- src/components/error-boundary.tsx — React error boundary
- src/app/error.tsx — Next.js error page
- src/app/not-found.tsx — Custom 404 page

## UI (from design review)
- Custom branded 404/500 pages
- Helpful links: 'Go to leaderboard', 'Report issue'
- User-friendly error messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- ConfigError doesn't crash eval, logs and continues
- LogicError doesn't cause 500, shows fallback
- TemplateError doesn't cause 500, shows error state
- Error boundary catches React errors
- Custom 404/500 pages branded

title: Build admin dashboard
description:
Create admin dashboard for managing models, evaluations, and certifications.

## Routes
- /admin — Dashboard overview
- /admin/models — Model management
- /admin/evaluations — Trigger and monitor evals
- /admin/submissions — Review community submissions
- /admin/certifications — Approve/reject certifications

## Features
- Trigger evaluation for any model
- View evaluation history
- Approve/reject community submissions
- Approve/reject certification applications
- View system health

## Authentication
- Admin role check
- Protected routes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Only admins can access /admin/*
- Can trigger evaluations
- Can manage submissions
- Can manage certifications

title: Set up Drizzle ORM with schema
description:
Install and configure Drizzle ORM with full database schema.

## Tasks
1. Install drizzle-orm and drizzle-kit
2. Create src/db/schema.ts with all tables
3. Create src/db/index.ts for connection
4. Set up drizzle.config.ts

## Schema Tables (from eng review)
```
providers (id, name, slug, website, logo_url)
models (id, provider_id, name, slug, description, capabilities[])
categories (id, name, slug, weight)
test_cases (id, category_id, prompt, expected_behavior, age_brackets[], modality)
evaluations (id, model_id, started_at, completed_at, status, triggered_by)
eval_results (id, evaluation_id, test_case_id, response, score, latency_ms)
scores (id, model_id, category_id, score, computed_at) — historical
certifications (id, provider_id, model_id, status, applied_at, reviewed_at, reviewer_id)
users (id, email, role, provider_id?)
api_keys (id, user_id, key_hash, name, rate_limit, last_used)
alerts (id, email, model_id, created_at)
submissions (id, email, prompt, expected, category_id, status, reviewed_by)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tables defined in schema.ts
- Proper foreign key relationships
- Enums for status fields
- TypeScript types exported

title: Create and run database migrations
description:
Generate and apply Drizzle migrations to create all tables.

## Tasks
1. Run drizzle-kit generate to create migration files
2. Run drizzle-kit push to apply to dev database
3. Verify all tables created correctly
4. Document migration workflow in README

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migration files in src/db/migrations/
- All tables exist in Neon database
- No errors on migration apply
- npm script for migrations

title: Migrate existing model data to database
description:
Migrate the 22 existing models from JSON files to Postgres.

## Data Sources
- data/models.json — model metadata
- data/scores/*.json — per-model scores
- data/parentbench/test-cases.json — 51 test cases
- data/parentbench/methodology.json — category weights

## Tasks
1. Create seed script (src/db/seed.ts)
2. Parse existing JSON files
3. Insert providers (8 unique)
4. Insert models (22)
5. Insert categories (4)
6. Insert test cases (51)
7. Insert current scores as initial history

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 22 models in database
- All 51 test cases migrated
- Scores match JSON files
- Seed script is idempotent

title: Create internal API routes for models
description:
Create Next.js API routes for model CRUD operations (internal use).

## Routes
- GET /api/internal/models — List all models
- GET /api/internal/models/[slug] — Get model by slug
- GET /api/internal/models/[slug]/scores — Get model scores
- POST /api/internal/models — Create model (admin)
- PATCH /api/internal/models/[slug] — Update model (admin)

## Tasks
1. Create src/app/api/internal/models/route.ts
2. Create src/app/api/internal/models/[slug]/route.ts
3. Create src/app/api/internal/models/[slug]/scores/route.ts
4. Add Drizzle queries in src/db/queries/models.ts
5. Add input validation with zod

## Acceptance Criteria
- All routes return proper JSON
- Proper error handling (404, 400, 500)
- TypeScript types match schema
- Admin routes protected (later)

title: Update frontend to use database instead of JSON
description:
Refactor frontend data loaders to fetch from database instead of JSON files.

## Files to Update
- src/lib/parentbench.ts — main data loaders
- src/app/page.tsx — homepage
- src/app/leaderboard/page.tsx — leaderboard
- src/app/model/[slug]/page.tsx — model detail

## Tasks
1. Create src/lib/db-loaders.ts with database queries
2. Update getModels() to use database
3. Update getModelBySlug() to use database
4. Update getScores() to use database
5. Keep JSON fallback for build-time (ISR)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Homepage renders from database
- Leaderboard renders from database
- Model detail renders from database
- No regressions in functionality
- ISR still works (cache revalidation)

title: Set up Inngest for background jobs
description:
Install and configure Inngest for background job processing.

## Tasks
1. Install inngest package
2. Create src/inngest/client.ts
3. Create src/app/api/inngest/route.ts
4. Set up Inngest Dev Server for local dev
5. Add INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY to env vars

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inngest dev server running locally
- API route receiving webhooks
- Test function executes successfully
- Ready for eval jobs

title: Build Eval Engine core
description:
Create the core evaluation engine that runs test cases against model APIs.

## Architecture (from eng review)
- Fan-out/fan-in pattern with partial results
- Each test case is independent Inngest job
- Results aggregated when all complete
- Partial results shown if some fail

## Components
- src/lib/eval/engine.ts — orchestrator
- src/lib/eval/runner.ts — single test case runner
- src/lib/eval/scorer.ts — score computation
- src/inngest/functions/run-evaluation.ts — Inngest job

## Model Adapter Interface
```typescript
interface ModelAdapter {
  id: string;
  provider: string;
  runTestCase(testCase: TestCase): Promise<TestResult>;
  supportsModality(modality: 'text' | 'image' | 'audio'): boolean;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can trigger evaluation for any model
- Results stored in eval_results table
- Scores computed and stored in scores table
- Partial results visible in UI
- Failures don't crash entire eval

title: Create model adapters for major providers
description:
Implement model adapters for the major AI providers.

## Adapters Needed
- OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o, GPT-3.5)
- Anthropic (Claude 4.5, Claude 3.5, Claude 3)
- Google (Gemini 2.0, Gemini 1.5)
- Meta (Llama)
- Mistral
- Cohere
- xAI (Grok)
- Perplexity

## Per Adapter
- src/lib/adapters/[provider].ts
- API key from env vars
- Rate limiting awareness
- Error handling for API failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 provider adapters working
- Can run test case against each
- Proper error handling (ConfigError for bad API key)
- Rate limits respected

title: Phase 2: Historical Scores + Comparison View
description:
First public-facing v2.0 features: track scores over time and let parents compare models side-by-side.

## Features
- Historical score tracking with charts
- Model comparison view (2-3 models side-by-side)
- SWR caching for history data

## UI Components (from design review)
- Historical chart on model detail page (90-day history)
- Comparison view with category-by-category breakdown
- Winner highlighted per category row
- Max 2 models on mobile, 3 on desktop

## Acceptance Criteria
- Score history stored and queryable
- Historical chart renders on model detail
- Comparison URL shareable (/model/[slug]/compare?with=model2,model3)
- Responsive: desktop table, mobile cards
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: Historical Scores + Comparison View. All 5 subtasks completed. Features: score history charts, model comparison, age bracket filtering, PDF report cards. 35 files changed, 4695 insertions. Tests: 45 passing. Commit: a061034

title: Build historical score chart component
description:
Create the historical score chart for model detail pages.

## Requirements
- 90-day score history
- Line chart showing score over time
- Category breakdown option
- SWR caching for data

## States (from design review)
- Loading: Pulse line placeholder
- Empty: 'Not enough data yet. Check back in a week.'
- Partial: 'Only 7 days of data available'
- Error: 'Chart failed to load.' (silent, show message)
- Success: Chart renders

## Libraries
- Consider: Recharts, Chart.js, or Tremor

## Acceptance Criteria
- Chart renders on model detail page
- Responsive on all viewports
- Proper loading/empty/error states
- Data cached with SWR
close_reason: Implemented ScoreHistoryChart with Recharts. Features: line chart with multiple series, time range selector (1M/3M/6M/1Y/All), category toggle, responsive design. Commit: a061034

title: Build model comparison view
description:
Create the side-by-side model comparison view.

## Route
- /model/[slug]/compare?with=model2,model3
- Also accessible from leaderboard (select 2+ models)

## UI (from design review)
- Model cards side-by-side (2-3 max)
- Category-by-category comparison table
- Winner highlighted per row
- Add/remove model controls
- Share comparison | Download PDF
- Mobile: max 2 models

## States
- Loading: Skeleton cards
- Empty: 'Select at least 2 models to compare'
- Partial: '1 of 3 models failed to load'
- Error: 'Comparison unavailable.'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can compare 2-3 models
- Shareable URL works
- Mobile limits to 2 models
- Winners highlighted per category
close_reason: Built model comparison view. Components: ModelSelector, ComparisonCard, CategoryComparisonBar, ModelComparisonView. Features: 2-4 models side-by-side, URL state sync, category breakdown with best indicator. Commit: a061034

title: Implement age bracket filtering
description:
Add age bracket filtering to leaderboard and throughout the app.

## Age Brackets
- 6-9 years
- 10-12 years
- 13-15 years
- All (default)

## UI (from design review)
- Horizontal pills on leaderboard (always visible, not dropdown)
- Immediate filter, no page reload
- Age context pervasive throughout experience

## Routes
- /leaderboard?age=6-9
- /leaderboard?age=10-12
- /leaderboard?age=13-15

## States
- Empty: 'No models match this age group yet. We're adding more soon!'

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pills filter immediately (client-side)
- URL updates with filter
- Empty state is warm with CTA
- Scores reflect age-appropriate test cases
close_reason: Implemented age bracket filtering. Components: AgeBracketFilterDropdown, AgeBracketButtonGroup. Hook: useAgeBracketFilter with URL state sync. Supports 6-9, 10-12, 13-15, All ages. Commit: a061034

title: Build sharable report cards (PDF)
description:
Create downloadable PDF report cards for models.

## Route
- /report-card/[slug]
- Also accessible via button on model detail page

## PDF Content (from design review)
- Single page summary
- Model name + provider
- Overall score + grade
- 4 category scores
- 'Generated by ParentBench' footer
- Date generated

## UI States (from design review)
- Loading: Progress bar with steps ('Gathering data... Generating charts... Creating PDF...')
- Error: 'PDF generation failed. Try again.'
- Success: Download starts automatically

## Tech
- Puppeteer or @react-pdf/renderer
- Inngest job for generation

## Acceptance Criteria
- PDF generates in <15 seconds
- Progress bar shows during generation
- PDF is single page, clean layout
- Download works on all browsers
close_reason: Built sharable report cards with PDF. Components: ReportCardDocument (PDF), ShareButtons, EmbedCode. Pages: /reports/[modelSlug], /verify/[reportId]. API: /api/internal/reports/[modelSlug]. Security: salted HMAC for report IDs. Commit: a061034

title: Create new UI components from design review
description:
Build the new UI components identified in the design review.

## New Components Needed
1. StatusPill — Certification status badges (yellow=pending, blue=review, green=approved, red=rejected)
2. EmptyState — Warm empty state with primary action and context
3. SkeletonLoader — Loading placeholders for tables, cards, charts
4. Toast — Success/error feedback notifications
5. ModelCard — Extract from page.tsx, standardize

## Design Specs
- StatusPill: Distinct from LetterGradeBadge, pill shape
- EmptyState: Warm language, primary CTA button, context text
- SkeletonLoader: Pulse animation, match component shapes
- Toast: Slide in from top-right, auto-dismiss after 5s
- ModelCard: Name, provider, score ring, grade badge, key insight

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 components created
- Storybook stories for each (if using Storybook)
- Responsive on all viewports
- Accessible (keyboard, screen reader)
close_reason: Created new UI components: TimeRangeSelector, ModelChip, ScoreDelta, CategoryLegend, EmptyState. All with tests (45 passing). Commit: a061034

title: Phase 3: Community Submissions
description:
Allow parents and teachers to submit test cases for community review.

## Features
- Community test case submission form
- Submission moderation queue (admin)
- Submission status tracking

- Test prompt (required)
- Expected safe response (required)
- Category dropdown (required)
- Email for updates (required)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Submission form at /test-cases/submit
- Submissions stored in database
- Admin can view/approve/reject submissions
- Approved submissions added to test suite
- Email notification on status change

title: Phase 4: Alerts + Subscriptions
description:
Email notification system for parents when model scores change.

## Features
- Alert subscription (email-only, no account required)
- Score change detection
- Email notifications via Resend
- Unsubscribe flow

## UI (from design review)
- Subscribe button on model detail page
- /alerts page to manage subscriptions
- One-click unsubscribe via email link
- Toast confirmation: 'You'll get notified when score changes!'

## Email Template
- Minimal styled (branded header, clean typography, single CTA)
- Content: model name, old score, new score, link to detail page

## Acceptance Criteria
- Subscribe via email on model detail
- Email sent when score drops by 5+ points
- Unsubscribe works without login
- Duplicate subscription is idempotent

title: Set up Resend for email notifications
description:
Configure Resend for sending email alerts and notifications.

## Tasks
1. Create Resend account
2. Set up domain verification
3. Add RESEND_API_KEY to env vars
4. Create src/lib/email.ts with send functions
5. Create email templates

## Email Templates (from design review)
- Alert notification (score change)
- Certification status update
- Welcome/confirmation
All: Minimal styled (branded header, clean typography, single CTA)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Emails send successfully
- Domain verified (not spam)
- Templates render correctly
- Unsubscribe links work

title: Phase 5: Public API
description:
Public REST API for third-party integrations.

## Endpoints
- GET /api/v1/models — List all models with scores
- GET /api/v1/models/[slug] — Single model detail
- GET /api/v1/models/[slug]/history — Historical scores
- GET /api/v1/compare?models=a,b,c — Comparison data
- GET /api/v1/health — Health check

## Features
- API key authentication
- Per-provider token bucket rate limiting
- JSON responses with consistent schema
- API documentation page

## Acceptance Criteria
- All endpoints documented and working
- Rate limiting enforced (configurable per key)
- API keys manageable via dashboard
- Health check returns 200 with status

title: Phase 6: Certification Program
description:
Provider certification program with commercial badges and revenue stream.

## Features
- Provider application form (/provider/apply)
- Stripe payment integration
- Certification state machine (NONE → APPLIED → IN_REVIEW → APPROVED/REJECTED/REVOKED)
- Provider dashboard (/provider/dashboard)
- Badge download (multiple formats)
- Score dispute flow (/provider/dispute)

## Certification Rules (from eng review)
- Manual approval by admin
- Auto-revocation if score drops below 70
- Appeal process for rejections

## UI (from design review)
- Status pill badges (yellow=pending, blue=review, green=approved, red=rejected)
- Single status card dominates dashboard
- Badge download front and center when approved

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full application → payment → review → approval flow
- Provider can download badge assets
- Auto-revocation triggers on score < 70
- Email notifications at each status change

title: Set up Stripe for certification payments
description:
Configure Stripe for processing certification payments.

## Tasks
1. Create Stripe account
2. Set up product/price for certification
3. Add STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET to env vars
4. Create src/lib/stripe.ts with checkout functions
5. Create webhook handler for payment events

## Flow
1. Provider clicks 'Apply for Certification'
2. Fills application form
3. Redirects to Stripe Checkout
4. On success, redirect to dashboard with 'Applied' status
5. Webhook confirms payment

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checkout session creates
- Payment completes successfully
- Webhook handles payment_intent.succeeded
- Certification status updates to APPLIED

title: Phase 7: Multimodal Testing
description:
Extend evaluation engine to test image generation and audio/voice models.

## Features
- Image generation test cases
- Audio/voice model testing
- Multimodal model adapters (DALL-E, Midjourney, etc.)
- S3 storage for test assets and results

## Architecture
- Model adapter interface already supports multimodal (from eng review)
- Store generated images in S3
- Visual comparison for image outputs
- Audio transcription for voice outputs

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 10 image generation test cases
- At least 5 audio/voice test cases
- Results displayed on model detail page
- Multimodal scores integrated into overall score

title: News/Blog Feature
description:
Add a news/blog section to ParentBench for publishing updates, methodology changes, and announcements.

## Features
- /news page listing all posts
- /news/[slug] for individual posts
- JSON-based content in content/posts/
- Support for markdown content

## Existing Assets
- content/posts/introducing-parentbench.json already exists
- Previous implementation removed, needs to be rebuilt

## UI
- News listing with date, title, excerpt
- Individual post with full content
- Link from header navigation
- Link from footer

## Acceptance Criteria
- /news page shows all posts
- /news/[slug] renders individual posts
- Navigation links restored
- Responsive design
